what_it_does: This file is a descriptive terrain-entry ledger record for the vi-visual-bridge project, also known as Vi-Wizard and the npm package vi-visual-frontend. It documents the project's purpose, server architecture, fabric-only connection to the Company front interface, naming relationships, and explicit separation from the vi-visual-dev factory repo.
observations: The project carries three coexisting names: folder vi-visual-bridge, product Vi-Wizard, and npm package vi-visual-frontend.
It is not a Chrome extension because no manifest.json exists; it is a bridge server plus MCP server plus an embedded Chrome single-page UI.
The MCP server runs on port 7732 and the bridge server on port 7731, deployed via ./deploy.sh.
Its relation to company is declared connected but fabric-only, with zero code imports from /home/tim/company.
Connection evidence is drawn from FORMATION_JOURNAL entries 13, 14, 21, and 68, describing a lead re-pointing the lane at the Company front interface through a company-channel fabric.
The framing is islands-join-mainland: build good parts into the centre and drop parallel scaffolding.
Code commits stopped on 2026-03-29, but uncommitted session activity in the journal ran to 2026-06-22.
The repo is 1.3 GB with 9,936 files, yet only 6 files were read for this entry.
vi-visual-dev is explicitly not this folder; it is a separate Windows-side factory repo at /mnt/c/00_ConceptV/06_Project_Vi/repos/vi-visual-dev with GitHub Concept-Vi/vi-vision.
The file maps three Vi layers: FACTORY/vi-visual-dev, DNA/counterpart, and this bridge/vi-visual-bridge.
standouts: Fabric-only company connection mediated through a lead and company-channel rather than code imports.
Islands-join-mainland absorption framing for the bridge's good parts into the Company centre.
Explicit name disambiguation: vi-visual-dev is a sibling factory repo, not this bridge.
concerns: Coverage is only 6 of 9,936 files, so most of the repo was not inspected.
Last active date mixes uncommitted journal activity with dormant code commits.
The connected relation rests on interpretive fabric evidence rather than verifiable code references.
The 1.3 GB size is dominated by node_modules and may not reflect source significance.
Multiple aliases and sibling repos create ongoing identity risk.
notes: A fabric-only bridge project ledger that carefully separates itself from the vi-visual-dev factory repo and frames its future as absorption into the Company front interface.
questions: What concrete endpoints or protocols does the bridge server expose beyond the listed ports?
What exactly is the Company front interface/centre and how would the bridge's parts be absorbed into it?
Are the fabric messages from the lead via company-channel bidirectional or purely directional?
What is the current deployment or runtime state of the bridge and MCP servers?
Why did code commits stop in March while journal activity continued into June?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is presented as a descriptive terrain-entry and it delivers descriptive documentation of the project's identity, architecture, fabric-only company relation, and naming disambiguation.
apparent_themes: Identity and name reconciliation across aliases and sibling repos.
Polymorphic visual interaction between AI agents and a human operator.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to position vi-visual-bridge/Vi-Wizard as a bridge whose useful components should be folded into the Company front interface, while preventing it from being mistaken for the separate vi-visual-dev factory repo or treated as a standalone parallel app.
novelty: A visual bridge packaged as a frontend bundle with an embedded Chrome UI, whose relation to a larger system is evidenced by agent journal entries and fabric coordination rather than code imports or extension manifests.
contribution: It contributes a precise identity map for three Vi layers, a documented fabric-only path connecting the bridge to the Company front interface, and an explicit islands-join-mainland framing for how the bridge's components should be integrated rather than duplicated.
summary_for_embedding: Descriptive terrain-entry for the Vi-Wizard / vi-visual-bridge project, a polymorphic visual interaction bridge server and MCP server with an embedded Chrome UI, documenting its architecture, fabric-only relation to the Company front interface, and distinction from the vi-visual-dev factory repo.
intention_for_embedding: To record and reconcile the project's identity, architecture, and fabric-level company connection so it can be oriented within the Company terrain without conflating it with sibling repositories.
